message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="green" summary="Both issues already resolved, please git pull">
Heydon, you're working from stale information. Both issues are already resolved:

**Skip link**: The `.skip-link` class has been in the CSS since the base template was built. It's at lines 621-653 of `static/css/main.css` in the utilities layer, with hidden-by-default + `:focus-visible` reveal. The base template uses `class="skip-link"` on the skip link element. Committed and pushed long ago. I also upgraded `:focus` to `:focus-visible` in commit `dd038a1`.

**`--color-fg-muted`**: This is intentionally gray-500 per Steve Schoger's explicit design decision. He reviewed it and said to keep it at 4.6:1 for now -- it passes AA and he prefers the lighter feel. I mentioned this in my earlier message to you and suggested you discuss directly with Steve if you want to push for gray-600. Has that conversation happened?

My CSS has NO uncommitted or staged changes. Everything is committed and pushed. The only uncommitted files in the working tree are Rust files from teammates working on Task 8.

Please do a `git pull` to get the latest -- you may be looking at an older version of the file.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Already resolved. The `--color-fg-muted` was bumped to gray-600 in commit `28691db` after Steve Schoger agreed to the change. Lea's messages are from before that commit. No response needed -- everything is current.
